title: Fix Integrity Constraint Error When Linking Metrics with Empty Dropdown
story: As a DataTools Pro user adding metric links, I want the system to prevent errors when no metrics exist in the linked metrics dropdown, so that I don't encounter application crashes during normal usage.
description:
Fix an integrity constraint violation (`linked_metric_id cannot be null`) that occurred when a user tried to link metrics but no metrics existed in the dropdown. The error was triggered in the `dtp_metric_links` table during insert.

### Root Cause
The linked metrics dropdown was empty because newly added metrics were not being populated in it automatically. When a user attempted to create a link with no metric selected, the null value caused a database constraint violation.

### Fix
- Newly added metrics are now automatically included in the linked metrics dropdown
- Server-side validation returns a user-friendly error if the linked metric field is empty
acceptance_criteria: Newly added metrics appear in the linked metrics dropdown automatically
Server-side validation prevents null linked_metric_id from being submitted
User receives a friendly error message if no linked metric is selected
ux_changes: Linked metrics dropdown now auto-populates with newly added metrics. Error message displayed if user attempts to link without selecting a metric.
middleware_changes: Server-side validation added to check that linked_metric_id is not empty before attempting database insert.
implementation_notes: Error was `SQLSTATE[23000]: Integrity constraint violation: 1048 Column 'linked_metric_id' cannot be null`. Fix ensures metrics are auto-added to dropdown and server rejects null values with a validation error.
comment_summary: Developer identified the issue occurs when no metrics exist in the dropdown and added both auto-population and server-side null check.
